Drivers: net investment income +$26.7M (+18%, higher yields and higher average invested assets); premiums +$17.4M (+2%, IIF growth and Enact Re GSE CRT participation, partially offset by higher ceded premiums); losses incurred +$12.1M unfavorable ($196M reserve release in 2024 vs $188M in 2023, outweighed by higher current-period loss expense of $209M from 34,820 new delinquencies vs $188M from 29,911 in 2023); $10.9M loss on debt extinguishment from redemption of the 2025 Notes (no comparable prior-year item); acquisition and operating expenses +$2.1M driven by severance from restructuring.","Let me pull the consolidated results for the nine-month periods.Let me get the clean data by separating the actual values from the changes, and also look at management's discussion.Now let me search management's discussion for context on the drivers.Let me also look for restructuring charges mentioned in the narrative.Here is the full breakdown of Enact's net income year-over-year change for the nine months ended September 30, 2024 compared to the same period in 2023, based on the Q3 2024 10-Q (ACT).
## Net Income Bridge: Nine Months Ended Sep 30, 2024 vs 2023
*(All dollar figures in thousands)*

column6: Insurance in-force growth + higher assumed premiums from Enact Re's GSE credit risk transfer participation, partially offset by higher ceded premiums. Net earned premium rate edged down to 0.36% from 0.37%.
Higher yields from elevated interest rates combined with higher average invested assets.
Realized losses on sales of fixed-maturity securities as part of a yield optimization strategy.

New primary delinquencies increased to 34,820 (vs. 29,911), contributing $209M in loss expense vs. $188M. Favorable reserve releases were $196M (2024) vs. $188M (2023), with decreased claim-rate assumptions due to sustained cure performance. The net effect was higher incurred losses.
Driven primarily by severance costs from restructuring activities.
Largely unchanged — relates to the 2025 Notes and 2029 Notes.
New item in 2024: expenses from redeeming the 2025 Notes after issuing $750M of 2029 Notes in May 2024.
Effective tax rate of 21.6% (2024) vs. 22.0% (2023), consistent with the U.S. federal corporate rate.

column1: ## What Drove the +$17.1M Increase in Net Income

**Revenue tailwinds (+$42.5M):** The single biggest driver was a **+$26.7M jump in net investment income**, fueled by higher portfolio yields and larger average invested assets. **Premium growth (+$17.4M)** also contributed, fed by insurance-in-force expansion and assumed GSE CRT premiums from Enact Re.
**Expense headwinds (-$24.1M):** Two items in particular weighed on the bottom line:
1. **Loss on debt extinguishment ($10.9M)** — a non-recurring charge from redeeming the 2025 Notes following the May 2024 $750M 2029 Notes issuance.

2. **Higher losses incurred (+$12.1M)** — more new delinquencies drove $209M in current-year loss expense (vs. $188M), partially offset by a $196M reserve release (vs. $188M in 2023) driven by continued favorable cure performance.

Despite these drags, strong top-line growth — particularly the investment income uplift from the higher-rate environment — powered net income from **$508.2M to $525.3M**, a **3.4% increase**.",True,"All key figures match ($525.3M vs $508.2M, +$17.1M, +3%), and all major drivers with dollar impacts are accurately identified and quantified.",0.007282,0.001772,"[{""tool"": ""search_concepts"", ""tokens_est"": 289}, {""tool"": ""search_concepts"", ""tokens_est"": 143}, {""tool"": ""search_concepts"", ""tokens_est"": 397}, {""tool"": ""query_financials"", ""tokens_est"": 2063}, {""tool"": ""query_financials"", ""tokens_est"": 512}, {""tool"": ""query_financials"", ""tokens_est"": 903}, {""tool"": ""search_narrative"", ""tokens_est"": 1494}, {""tool"": ""search_narrative"", ""tokens_est"": 1402}, {""tool"": ""search_narrative"", ""tokens_est"": 1495}, {""tool"": ""search_narrative"", ""tokens_est"": 1100}]",9798,77.87
